title: B1 Listening Part 1 - Test 46
instructions: For each question, listen to the recording and choose the correct answer: A, B or C.

items.context: You hear two people talking about a lost phone.
items.audio_script: Man: Did you finally find your phone? You looked everywhere before class this morning. || Boy: Yes, but not where I expected. I checked the cafe, because I remembered using it there yesterday. || Man: And did you try the bus? You said you went there afterwards. || Boy: I did, but nobody had seen it. Then the shop assistant sent me a message and said [1]I had left my phone under the table by the window[/1]. I must have put it there without noticing. || Man: What a relief. You will need it later. || Boy: Exactly. I'll pick it up before lunch.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you finally find your phone? You looked everywhere before class this morning.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Yes, but not where I expected. I checked the cafe, because I remembered using it there yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: And did you try the bus? You said you went there afterwards.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: I did, but nobody had seen it. Then the shop assistant sent me a message and said I had left my phone under the table by the window. I must have put it there without noticing.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What a relief. You will need it later.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Exactly. I'll pick it up before lunch.

items.question: Where is the phone?
items.explanation: The speaker says they had left the phone under the table by the window.

items.context: You hear two friends making plans for the afternoon.
items.audio_script: Woman: Are we still going to visit the street market after school? I was looking forward to it. || Man: I don't think so. Most stalls are closed today, so that plan will not work today. || Woman: We could go to the aquarium instead. That might be fun. || Man: I checked, but tickets have sold out. My cousin had a different idea: [1]we are going to take photos in the park this afternoon[/1]. || Woman: That sounds good. Shall we meet near the station? || Man: Yes, at four o'clock. Then we can go together before dinner.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are we still going to visit the street market after school? I was looking forward to it.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I don't think so. Most stalls are closed today, so that plan will not work today.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: We could go to the aquarium instead. That might be fun.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I checked, but tickets have sold out. My cousin had a different idea: we are going to take photos in the park this afternoon.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds good. Shall we meet near the station?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, at four o'clock. Then we can go together before dinner.

items.question: What are the friends going to do?
items.explanation: The speaker says they are going to take photos in the park this afternoon.

items.context: You hear two students talking about the beach clean-up.
items.audio_script: Girl: Have you packed for the beach clean-up tomorrow? I'm trying not to take things I will not use. || Boy: Almost. I was going to bring sandals, but the teacher said we will not need that there. || Girl: What about a beach ball? Do we need that? || Boy: Not this time. The message says [1]everyone needs to bring strong gloves because we may pick up sharp pieces of rubbish[/1]. || Girl: Thanks for telling me. I will put it in my bag now. || Boy: Good idea. We leave early, and there will not be time to go back home.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Have you packed for the beach clean-up tomorrow? I'm trying not to take things I will not use.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Almost. I was going to bring sandals, but the teacher said we will not need that there.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: What about a beach ball? Do we need that?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Not this time. The message says everyone needs to bring strong gloves because we may pick up sharp pieces of rubbish.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Thanks for telling me. I will put it in my bag now.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Good idea. We leave early, and there will not be time to go back home.

items.question: What should the student take?
items.explanation: The speaker says everyone needs to bring strong gloves because we may pick up sharp pieces of rubbish.

items.context: You hear two people choosing food in a cafe.
items.audio_script: Woman: Have you chosen what to order for lunch? The queue is getting longer behind us. || Man: Not yet. I considered noodles, but they are too hot. || Woman: What about fried rice? It looks good today. || Man: I thought about that too, but it is already cold. I think [1]I'll have the vegetable wrap because I can eat it quickly[/1]. || Woman: Good choice. I'll decide while you pay. || Man: Fine. Let's find seats by the window before all the tables are taken. We still have time before class begins.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you chosen what to order for lunch? The queue is getting longer behind us.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Not yet. I considered noodles, but they are too hot.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What about fried rice? It looks good today.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I thought about that too, but it is already cold. I think I'll have the vegetable wrap because I can eat it quickly.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Good choice. I'll decide while you pay.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Fine. Let's find seats by the window before all the tables are taken. We still have time before class begins.

items.question: What food does the speaker choose?
items.explanation: The speaker chooses the vegetable wrap because I can eat it quickly.

items.context: You hear two students talking about the cooking group.
items.audio_script: Boy: Did you see the message about the cooking group this afternoon? I was going straight to the kitchen classroom. || Girl: Do not go there, because a new oven is being installed. The teacher cannot use that room today. || Boy: Oh. I heard some people were waiting by the canteen. || Girl: That is wrong too, because lunch is still being served. The latest message says [1]we must meet in the art room after lunch[/1]. || Boy: Thanks. I would have gone to the wrong place. || Girl: I will tell the others before registration, so nobody misses the start.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Did you see the message about the cooking group this afternoon? I was going straight to the kitchen classroom.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Do not go there, because a new oven is being installed. The teacher cannot use that room today.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Oh. I heard some people were waiting by the canteen.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: That is wrong too, because lunch is still being served. The latest message says we must meet in the art room after lunch.

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Thanks. I would have gone to the wrong place.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: I will tell the others before registration, so nobody misses the start.

items.question: Where should students go for the cooking group?
items.explanation: The latest message says they must meet in the art room after lunch.

items.context: You hear two people talking about travelling to the town library.
items.audio_script: Woman: How are you getting to the town library tomorrow? I thought you were going to drive. || Man: I was, but Mum needs the car for work, so it is not a good idea now. || Woman: Could you cycle instead? It might be quicker. || Man: I checked, but it may rain heavily. [1]I'm walking because it only takes fifteen minutes[/1]. || Woman: That makes sense. When will you arrive? || Man: Just before ten. I will send you a message when I get there, and we can meet near the entrance.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How are you getting to the town library tomorrow? I thought you were going to drive.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I was, but Mum needs the car for work, so it is not a good idea now.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Could you cycle instead? It might be quicker.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I checked, but it may rain heavily. I'm walking because it only takes fifteen minutes.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That makes sense. When will you arrive?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Just before ten. I will send you a message when I get there, and we can meet near the entrance.

items.question: How will the speaker get to the town library?
items.explanation: The speaker chooses this way of travelling because it only takes fifteen minutes.

items.context: You hear two people talking about work at the cinema.
items.audio_script: Woman: What are you doing at the cinema this afternoon? I thought you had to sell popcorn. || Girl: That was yesterday. Someone else has already done it this morning. || Woman: So are you going to check tickets instead? || Girl: No, because the film starts much later. The manager said [1]my job today is to tidy the seats after the film[/1]. || Woman: That sounds manageable. Will you finish by five? || Girl: I hope so. If it stays quiet, I should finish early and have time to meet my friends afterwards.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What are you doing at the cinema this afternoon? I thought you had to sell popcorn.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: That was yesterday. Someone else has already done it this morning.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So are you going to check tickets instead?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: No, because the film starts much later. The manager said my job today is to tidy the seats after the film.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds manageable. Will you finish by five?

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: I hope so. If it stays quiet, I should finish early and have time to meet my friends afterwards.

items.question: What job will the speaker do?
items.explanation: The speaker says their job today is to tidy the seats after the film.
